fingerprint: The dome disc radius 20,015 km = πR_Earth to 0.0006% precision — the defining equation of the azimuthal equidistant projection. Every dome coordinate is a globe surface distance projected flat.

pi_times_R_earth.significance: This is the pole-to-antipode surface arc distance on a sphere — the AE projection radius. The disc IS the globe, unwrapped.

km_per_degree_111_1.dome_usage: find_curve.py line 34: r = (90 - lat) × 111.1 — the AE projection formula, using globe arc distance as the radial coordinate

dome_value: a = 20,015 km (semi-major axis / disc radius / 'ice-wall radius')
why_it_matters: The dome's most fundamental structural parameter — the size of the disc — is not independently derived from any dome physics. It is π × R_Earth, which is the mathematical definition of the azimuthal equidistant projection radius. This means every dome formula that uses a = 20,015 km (Tesla wave speed, Schumann resonance, gravity profile, solar elevation, distance calculations) is implicitly using globe geometry. The disc diameter 40,030 km matches the globe circumference by construction, not by coincidence — because the disc IS the globe's surface projected flat via AE mapping.

key_findings.note: Hardcoded as a literal constant. No derivation shown.

THE smoking gun. This is the AE projection formula: radial coordinate = colatitude × (globe_circumference / 360). The 111.1 km/degree IS the globe's surface geometry. At lat=-90 (antipode): r = 180 × 111.1 = 19,998 km ≈ 20,015 km.

key_findings.content: Baseline coords (V50.6) mapped accurately to WGS84
key_findings.note: Script header explicitly states the dome coordinates are calibrated to WGS84 distances.
The value 20,015 is used as a default and confirmed as 'optimal' — i.e., it minimizes error against WGS84 distances.

code_search.notes: No script derives 20,015 km from dome geometry. The value enters as a hardcoded constant (inject_ai_layer.py) or as the output of fitting to WGS84 distances (distance_analysis.py optimal_a_km). The find_curve.py formula r = (90-lat)×111.1 proves the coordinate system IS the AE projection of a globe.

additional_fingerprints_in_section.feature: The 45 km gap between disc diameter (40,030) and equatorial circumference (40,075)
additional_fingerprints_in_section.explanation: Our section says 40,030 is 'suspiciously close to' 40,075 (equatorial circumference). But the disc diameter actually matches the MERIDIONAL-derived value πR × 2 = 40,030.2 km, not the equatorial circumference. The 45 km difference IS the difference between the Earth's meridional and equatorial circumferences (oblateness). The dome's disc encodes the MERIDIONAL geometry because the AE projection maps along meridians (pole-to-equator-to-antipode), not along the equator.
additional_fingerprints_in_section.suggested_text_improvement: Replace 'suspiciously close to 40,075 km — Earth's equatorial circumference' with 'equal to 2πR_Earth within 0.001% — the exact value predicted by the azimuthal equidistant projection of a sphere with mean radius 6,371 km. The 45 km gap between the disc diameter and the equatorial circumference (40,075 km) is itself a fingerprint: it matches the difference between Earth's meridional and equatorial geometry, because the AE projection maps along meridians, not the equator.'

additional_fingerprints_in_section.feature: The 0.9941 EW angular scale factor (dome model page)
additional_fingerprints_in_section.explanation: The dome uses θ = −lon_E with a correction factor 0.9941. On a perfect AE projection, longitude maps directly to angle. The 0.9941 factor is an ad-hoc fudge to correct for the fact that the dome's elliptical disc (a=20,015, b=15,000) doesn't map longitude as cleanly as a circular AE projection. This is another globe leak: the dome needs a correction factor because globe longitude doesn't project perfectly onto an ellipse.

additional_fingerprints_in_section.feature: The ellipse parameters (a=20,015, b=15,000) are WGS84-calibrated
additional_fingerprints_in_section.explanation: distance_analysis.py computes dome distances and compares them against 'Real WGS84 distances.' The a=20,015 and b=15,000 values are the parameters that minimize error against globe-measured city-to-city distances. The dome's shape is literally fitted to globe data.

section_strengthening_suggestions: Make the πR_Earth identity explicit: 20,015 km = π × 6,371 km (0.0006% match). This transforms 'suspiciously close' into 'mathematically identical.'
Note that find_curve.py line 34 (r = (90-lat) × 111.1) is the AE projection formula using globe arc distances. The dome's coordinate system IS a globe coordinate system projected flat.

Correct the comparison target: the disc diameter matches the meridional-derived πR value (40,030.2 km), not the equatorial circumference (40,075 km). The 45 km gap is itself diagnostic of Earth's oblateness encoded in the projection.
